name: Versatile Cut-Out Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Combine the flour, baking powder, and salt. In a large mixer bowl, beat the condensed milk, butter, eggs, and vanilla extract until well blended. Add the dry ingredients; mix well. Chill for 2 hours. Preheat oven to 350°F; grease cookie sheets. On a floured surface, knead dough to form a smooth ball. Divide into thirds. On a well-floured surface, roll out each portion to 1/8-inch thick. Cut with floured cookie cutters. Reroll as necessary to use all of the dough. Place cookies about 1 inch apart on the sheets. Bake for 7 to 9 minutes, or until lightly browned around the edges. Do not overbake. Cool. Frost and decorate as desired. Store loosely covered at room temperature.
Variations:
- Chocolate Cookies: Decrease flour to 3 cups. Add 1/2 cup unsweetened cocoa. Proceed as above. Makes 6 1/2 dozen.
- Mincemeat Peek-a-Boo Cookies: Prepare and roll the dough as above. Cut into 3-inch rounds. Using a sharp knife, cut an X in half of the rounds. Place 1 teaspoon mincemeat in the center of the remaining rounds. Top with the cut rounds; press edges to seal. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes. Cool. Sprinkle with powdered sugar if desired. Makes 4 dozen.
- Stained Glass Cookies: Prepare and roll the dough as above. Using 3-inch cookie cutters, cut into desired shapes. Cut out holes for "stained glass" in each cookie with small cutters or a knife. Place on aluminum foil-lined sheets. Fill the holes with crushed hard candies. If planning to hang cookies, make a hole near the edge with a straw. Bake for 6 to 8 minutes, or until candy has melted. Cool for 10 minutes; remove from foil. Makes 8 dozen.
- Cinnamon Pinwheel Cookies: Decrease baking powder to 2 tablespoons. Prepare and chill dough as above. Divide into quarters. Roll each quarter of dough into a 16 x 8-inch rectangle. Brush with melted butter or margarine. Top each with 2 tablespoons sugar combined with 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon. Roll up tightly beginning at the 8-inch side. Wrap tightly; freeze for about 20 minutes until firm. Cut into 1/4-inch thick slices. Place on ungreased baking sheets. Bake for 12 to 14 minutes, or until lightly browned. Makes 6 1/2 dozen.
